mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 5.5 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicates broad-based demand expansion, which increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the industrial production index above 105 reflects capacity utilization peaking, which historically precedes margin compression and earnings revisions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of observations where INDPRO exceeds 105, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading exceeding 2.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity risk premiums and forces multiple contraction, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns are positive in more than 50 percent of triggered episodes, or the signal fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold benchmark over a 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, which mechanically supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling Rank IC drops below 0.02 for three consecutive years, or forward returns in positive GDP growth quarters are not statistically distinct from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales and employment indicate strengthening consumer spending power, which drives revenue growth for consumer-facing equities and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression, or forward returns fail to exceed the sample median in more than 55 percent of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 105 while housing starts decline, it signals a demand shift away from residential investment toward industrial overcapacity, which triggers earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive forward returns in over 60 percent of instances, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.1 over a 2-year out-of-sample test.
